title: senecio protractus
intro: Senecio protractus, also known as ragwort, is a perennial plant species native to New Zealand and parts of the Pacific Islands. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving joint pain, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Senecio protractus is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although its use should be cautious due to potential toxicity.
